title: Fix first-admin auto-promotion and set admin password

requirements.summary: Set the first admin account's password to 'Munnu1998@' (SHA-256 hashed before sending to backend)
acceptanceCriteria: On the AdminLoginPage, logging in with the password 'Munnu1998@' (hashed via SHA-256 before the backend call, consistent with useAdminAuth.ts) successfully authenticates the first admin.
The admin can access the AdminDashboardPage after logging in with this password.

file_operations.description: Update the AdminLoginPage to pre-fill or document the password field with 'Munnu1998@' during first-admin registration flow. When the first-admin registration is triggered (adminExists === false), either auto-populate the password field or display a prominent note instructing the user to use 'Munnu1998@' as the initial password. Ensure the password is SHA-256 hashed via useAdminAuth before calling the backend's verifyAuthentication method. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Update AdminGuard and AdminLoginPage first-admin registration flow to complete successfully without 'Unauthorized' error
acceptanceCriteria: When no admin exists, the AdminGuard shows the first-admin registration prompt and submitting it does not produce an 'Unauthorized' error.
The flow completes and the user is recognized as admin after registration.
No 'Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles' error appears during the first-admin setup flow.

file_operations.description: Review the AdminGuard component's first-admin registration flow. When adminExists is false, ensure the component calls useAdminRegistration's registerFirstAdmin mutation instead of any role-assignment flow that requires prior admin authorization. Remove or bypass any adminCheck guard during the first-admin registration flow. After successful first-admin registration, invalidate admin-related queries (isAdmin, adminExists) so the user is immediately recognized as admin. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure the first-admin registration flow on AdminLoginPage calls useAdminRegistration's registerFirstAdmin mutation when no admin exists. Do not call any role-assignment or invitation methods during first-admin setup. After successful registration, automatically trigger a login attempt using the pre-seeded password 'Munnu1998@' (SHA-256 hashed). On success, navigate to AdminDashboardPage. Add error handling to display user-friendly messages if registration fails. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Review the useAdminRegistration hook's registerFirstAdmin mutation. Ensure it correctly calls actor.registerFirstAdmin() without requiring prior admin authorization. After successful registration, invalidate ['isAdmin'], ['adminExists'], and ['adminUserCount'] query keys so subsequent checks recognize the new admin. Add comprehensive error handling and return success/failure status. The hook should not call assignRole or inviteAdminUser during first-admin registration.
